title: #188 - Förvärvare eller fördärvare?
summary_md:
# #188 - Förvärvare eller fördärvare?

**Feed**: affarsvarlden-analys  
**Published**: 2026-06-16  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

The final pre-summer episode of Affärsvärlden Analys reviews the weak first half for stock-pickers — over 300 of their year-old calls trailed a strong broad index by ~14% on average, with small-caps lagging big-caps. The dominant theme is serial acquirers: half of 2026's six IPOs are roll- ups, and the hosts weigh the AI threat to software compounders against cheap valuations and acquisition arbitrage. Concrete calls include subscribe/buy on Nordtech, Vitec, Vestum and Tången, a sell on the premium-valued investment company Öresund, and a neutral stance on Scandic ahead of its Dalata deal.

---

## Universe Stocks

### 🟢 VIT-B — Vitec | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Much larger, European-expanding software serial acquirer; hosts hold a buy rating and see it as somewhat cheaper than peers.

#### Reasoning

Hosts reaffirm a buy on Vitec, noting it is comparatively cheap given the software de-rating. It is far larger than Nordtech and has expanded acquisitions into the Netherlands. Same AI overhang on software compounders applies, but valuation already reflects much of the fear.

#### Sell Triggers

- if AI threat compresses software multiples further
- if acquisition pace or returns slow
- if organic growth turns negative

> *"Vi har köpråd på Vitek också. Apropå att de var lite billigare."*

---

### 🔴 ORES — Öresund | **SELL** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 70%

Investment company trading at ~20% premium to NAV; 90%+ listed holdings, no buy ratings on any holding, weak long-run track record.

#### Reasoning

NAV is ~SEK 131/share vs SEK 151 price — a ~20% premium with no justification: holdings are 96% listed (so NAV isn't understated), the portfolio has underperformed the index for most of the past decade, management fees are 0.5-1%, and there is no privileged deal access. Hosts see no reason to pay a premium and reiterate a sell despite a painful sell call a year ago.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the premium converts to a discount
- if Scandi Standard or other holdings re-rate sharply
- if a value-style market rotation lifts the portfolio

> *"Slutsatsen är ganska enkel, att vi landar i ett säljråd. För vi ser ingen skäl att handla Öresund till nästan 20 procents premie."*

---

### 🟢 VESTUM — Vestum | **BUY** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 65%

Turnaround serial acquirer that sold its Water/pump division for SEK 6.5bn (SEK 3.5bn gain), paying a large special dividend; remainder cheap.

#### Reasoning

After a debt crisis, Vestum stabilized and built a Water unit it now sells to Nordic Capital at ~5x sales/20x EBIT. It distributes most proceeds (SEK 13.50 special dividend) rather than empire-building. The remaining ~SEK 2.2bn-revenue business trades around EV/EBIT 10-11 with margin upside as a turnaround; founder Conny Ryk returns as CEO.

#### Sell Triggers

- if the retained operations fail to improve margins
- if Ryk resumes aggressive debt-funded M&A
- if proceeds are not returned as promised

> *"Vi satte faktiskt ett köpråd på det."*

---

### 🟡 SHOT — Scandic | **HOLD** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Neutral | **Time Horizon**: MT  
**Confidence**: 55%

Nordic's largest hotel operator buying Dalata's operations for EUR 500m, taking on ~SEK 5.5bn debt; cheap vs peers but integration and disclosure risk.

#### Reasoning

Hosts hold a neutral rating. Scandic looks optically cheap (~11x EBIT / 12x earnings on 2027 estimates) but its lease-heavy operator model justifies a discount to franchise-based global chains. The Dalata deal could lift revenue from SEK 22bn toward 30bn, but figures are stale (2024), the price looks high, and large transformative deals usually prove messy and costly. Top owner Eiendomspar selling half its Scandic stake is a cautionary signal.

#### Sell Triggers

- if Dalata integration costs or synergies disappoint
- if Dalata's actual figures come in below 2024 levels
- if leverage strains the balance sheet

> *"Vi hade neutralt råd istället."*

---

## Summary Table

| Ticker | Company | Signal | TH | Conf | Context |
|--------|---------|--------|----|------|---------|
| **VIT-B** | Vitec | 🟢 BUY | LT | 60% | Much larger, European-expanding software serial acquirer; hosts hold a buy rating and see it as somewhat cheaper than peers. |
| **ORES** | Öresund | 🔴 SELL | MT | 70% | Investment company trading at ~20% premium to NAV; 90%+ listed holdings, no buy ratings on any holding, weak long-run track record. |
| **VESTUM** | Vestum | 🟢 BUY | MT | 65% | Turnaround serial acquirer that sold its Water/pump division for SEK 6.5bn (SEK 3.5bn gain), paying a large special dividend; remainder cheap. |
| **SHOT** | Scandic | 🟡 HOLD | MT | 55% | Nordic's largest hotel operator buying Dalata's operations for EUR 500m, taking on ~SEK 5.5bn debt; cheap vs peers but integration and disclosure risk. |
